title: Umami Velvety Moroccan Lentil Harira
description: A comforting Moroccan dish featuring dashi and nuka-pickle for a rich umami flavor, this satvik-friendly harira is a perfect blend of fermented and velvety textures. It is a hearty and comforting meal for any time of the day.
instructions: In a large pot, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for another minute, stirring constantly.
Add the red lentils, dashi broth, nuka-pickle brine, tomato paste, salt, and black pepper to the pot.
Stir well to combine, then bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 30 minutes, or until the lentils are tender.
Use an immersion blender to puree the harira to a velvety texture, or allow it to cool and puree it in a blender.
Serve the harira hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs or a dollop of yogurt, if desired.
